title: Light Bitter Russian Tomatoes Pilaf
description: A light and refreshing one-pot dish featuring vine tomatoes and the smoky flavor of tandoor, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This plant-based-friendly recipe is easy to make and packed with nutrients.

ingredients.name: tandoor-smoked tomatoes
ingredients.notes: chopped, fresh from the garden or store-bought

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large tandoor or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the grated carrots and sliced zucchini. Cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped tandoor-smoked tomatoes, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Add the white rice to the pot and stir to coat the rice with the oil and mix with the vegetables.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the lemon juice and chopped green onions. Serve hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
Let the pilaf rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
